name: Smoked Turkey with Smoked Oyster Dressing
recipeIngredient: 1 loaf stale bread, pulled into chunks
recipeInstructions:
This is a fairly straightforward smoked turkey recipe.
Prepare your smoker and smoke the turkey for six to eight hours between 220-240 degrees. Use hardwood (a 50/50 mix of apple and oak is excellent if available) or include at least some fruit wood. If in a dry climate, add a water pan underneath or between the turkey and heat source to ensure moisture.
When ready to finish cooking the turkey, remove it from the smoker. Place it in a large Dutch oven with 1 cup white vinegar and about 6-7 cups water (enough liquid for 1.5-2 inches at bottom). Optionally sprinkle garlic powder, black pepper, and drizzle honey. Bake at 325 degrees for 2-3 hours or until internal temperature reaches 190 degrees.
For the dressing, lightly brush a large baking pan with oil and add stale bread chunks. About 15 minutes before turkey is done, stir in chopped onion, celery, garlic, jalapeno, sage, oregano, pepper, mustard seeds, smoked oysters, and reserved oyster liquid. Combine well.
Add 2-3 cups of turkey liquor from the Dutch oven and 4 tablespoons worcestershire sauce to the dressing. Mix until doughy but not wet. Bake at 325 degrees for about 45 minutes to an hour or until top is slightly golden brown. Serve dressing alongside the cooked turkey.
